response.justification: Strong geospatial domain expertise (GISCollective founder, Green Map, FOSS4G speaker) and distributed systems knowledge provide valuable background for routing pipelines. However, commercial C++ experience ended ~10 years ago with recent focus on TypeScript/Node.js web development, requiring significant ramping on modern systems programming. Demonstrated learning agility and full-stack mindset suggest potential capability to refresh skills, but the gap on the primary technical requirement (modern C++) and lack of explicit routing/navigation pipeline experience create meaningful risk for a critical production role.
strengths: Founded GISCollective (open-source geospatial platform)—rare domain expertise directly applicable to mapping and routing systems
Proven distributed systems experience handling millions of requests/second with real-time async I/O and caching at Dunnhumby
Demonstrated learning velocity across multiple languages and domains (C++, DLang, Rust-adjacent work, web, embedded, DevOps infrastructure)
concerns: Last commercial C++ work was 2014-2015; recent decade focused on TypeScript/Node.js web development rather than systems programming
No explicit experience building or operating routing data pipelines, tile-based systems, or navigation guidance infrastructure at scale
No Rust experience despite job listing it as an acceptable alternative to C++

key_factors.factor: Geospatial domain expertise (GISCollective, Green Map, mapping systems)
10-year gap in commercial C++ since 2015; web-focused recent career trajectory
Distributed systems and real-time async systems experience
